inputs.attackFlagText: Boundary inclusivity ('by height h+W') is not pinned in spec text — <= vs < differs by one block and only the sim's <= exists (see D13).
inputs.predicateSignature: daVerdict(anchor, firstServableHeight, currentHeight, W[#49 provisional=2], C[#49 provisional=3], K[#49 provisional=6], priorKernelState) -> {verdict}

fixture.caseFirstServableAtHplusW: a claim whose first-servable height is exactly h+W (provisional W=2) -> in-window -> ACCEPT (holds priority): DA agreement §6e S2 'a witness at the deadline height exactly is in-window'
fixture.caseOneBlockLate: the documenting companion: first-servable = h+W+1 -> out-of-window (reject); this pins the inclusive <= boundary against a strict-< reading
fixture.WCK: #49 provisional consensus parameters (W=2, C=3, K=6); §6e S2 inclusive-deadline reading
fixture.priorState: a single in-window claim on an uncontested name

inputs.scopeNote: Originated provisional on da-windows (#49, RATIFIED 2026-06-14): the verdict (a claim first-servable at exactly h+W is in-window) is derivable, but the inclusive (<= vs <) boundary reading is pinned by #49 S2, which is RATIFIED 2026-06-14. This is a DA-window edge (h+W) covered by #49 S2 — distinct from the non-DA window edges (notice-window/bond/collision/maturity) that route to PR-13. Authored with provisional W=2/C=3/K=6; flips to reject under a strict-< ruling under the ratified branch. originated as a provisional-vector.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored from a provisional vector (decisionDeps + flipMarker shed on ratification); status locked (per-area adversarial review passed; authorityTier RATIFIED 2026-06-14 (DK ratified da-windows #49 O1 / recovery-auth #50 b1); lineage in DECISIONS #49/#50, registry Part B, decision papers, git); ratified per DK's #49 S2 boundary ruling.

expected.invariant: under #49 S2 inclusive deadlines a claim whose first-servable height is exactly h+W is in-window and accepted (holds priority); h+W+1 is out-of-window; identical on every deterministic replay. This is a DA-window edge (h+W) pinned by #49 S2, not one of the non-DA edges routed to PR-13.
expected.rationale: Boundary inclusivity (<= vs <) is now pinned by da-windows (#49) S2 in DA agreement §6e — 'By h+X' means height <= h+X, witness at the deadline height exactly is in-window. The verdict (first-servable = h+W is in-window) is derivable, but the algebra is ratified 2026-06-14 ratification, so this is provisional-origin, now ratified.

sources: docs/core/B2_KERNEL_HARDENING.md D3 reorg flag (line 291); Gaps 'Reorg semantics of the verdict' (line 409)
docs/core/DECISIONS.md 49. da-windows S1 (lines 1057-1059) — reorgs re-derive h; RATIFIED 2026-06-14 (lines 1043-1047)
docs/spec/ONT_DATA_AVAILABILITY_AGREEMENT.md §6e S1 (lines 170-173) — 'On reorg, h re-derives from the new containing block'
docs/core/DECISIONS.md Open Question 6 (lines 1243-1248) — reorg handling is part of the W/C/K window design

inputs.attackFlagText: Reorg can change h itself; verdict behavior across an anchor reorg is unspecified (DECISIONS Open Question 6: 'Still open: reorg handling, which is the W/C/K window design').
inputs.predicateSignature: daVerdict(anchor, containingBlockHeight[re-derived on reorg], W, C, K, witnessedChainFacts{reorgHistory}, priorKernelState) -> {verdict}

fixture.caseStaleClockRejected: an anchor whose containing block is reorged out and re-mined at h': the deadlines (h'+W, h'+W+C) re-derive from h'; a verdict computed against the STALE pre-reorg h is non-conformant -> REJECT the stale-clock evaluation
fixture.caseReDerivedClockAccepts: the same anchor evaluated against the re-derived h' yields the correct, replay-stable verdict (the positive companion to the rejected stale-clock case)
fixture.clock: #49 S1: h is the mined height of the anchor's containing block in the evaluator's current best chain; on reorg h re-derives from the new containing block and every deadline moves with it
fixture.priorState: an anchor confirmed at h, then reorged out and re-mined at h' != h

inputs.scopeNote: Originated provisional on da-windows (#49, RATIFIED 2026-06-14): the reorg deadline-clock rule (h re-derives from the new containing block on reorg; deadlines move with it) is pinned by #49 S1, RATIFIED 2026-06-14. The verdict (a stale-pre-reorg-h evaluation is non-conformant; the correct verdict re-derives from h') is derivable from S1 but derives from the now-ratified decision. Broader reorg window-restart semantics beyond the clock remain a documented gap (Open Question 6); this vector tests only the S1 clock re-derivation. originated as a provisional-vector.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored from a provisional vector (decisionDeps + flipMarker shed on ratification); status locked (per-area adversarial review passed; authorityTier RATIFIED 2026-06-14 (DK ratified da-windows #49 O1 / recovery-auth #50 b1); lineage in DECISIONS #49/#50, registry Part B, decision papers, git); ratified per DK's #49 S1 reorg-clock ruling.

expected.invariant: the deadline clock h is the mined height of the anchor's containing block in the evaluator's current best chain (#49 S1); on reorg h re-derives from the new containing block and every deadline (h+W, h+W+C) moves with it — a verdict computed against the stale pre-reorg h is non-conformant and rejected; identical on deterministic replay over the reorg
expected.rationale: da-windows (#49) S1 now pins the reorg story for the deadline clock: h is the mined height of the anchor's containing block in the evaluator's current best chain, and 'On reorg, h re-derives from the new containing block; every deadline moves with it.' The verdict that deadlines key off the re-derived h is derivable, but the algebra is ratified 2026-06-14. (Broader reorg re-evaluation/window-restart semantics beyond the clock remain a documented gap, but the flag's claim — that across-reorg verdict behavior is unspecified — is now answered for the deadline clock by #49.)

sources: docs/core/B2_KERNEL_HARDENING.md D6 (lines 315-323)
docs/core/DECISIONS.md 49. da-windows S2/S3 (lines 1060-1064) — inclusive deadlines + holdsPriority keyed to h+W; RATIFIED 2026-06-14
docs/spec/ONT_DATA_AVAILABILITY_AGREEMENT.md §6d (lines 155-161) [candidate] — contested priority forfeit on missed hard deadline; §6e S3 (lines 177-181)
docs/spec/ONT_DATA_AVAILABILITY_AGREEMENT.md §3 (lines 80-89) — boundary fork hazard

inputs.attackFlagText: Selective reveal at the boundary: an attacker who arranges first-servable exactly at the deadline exploits D13's unpinned inclusivity to win on some implementations and lose on others — a convergence fork on a contested name, the worst case in §3.

fixture.caseJustPastDeadlineForfeits: on a contested name, a claimant whose first-servable height is h+W+1 (one block past the inclusive hard deadline) has missed the §6d hard deadline -> forfeits priority -> REJECT
fixture.caseBoundaryConverges: the companion convergence fact: a claimant first-servable exactly at h+W resolves identically (in-window, holds priority) on every verifier under #49 S2 — the boundary selective-reveal attack cannot manufacture a divergent outcome; a strict-< implementation that would treat h+W as out-of-window is the non-conformant reading
fixture.priorState: a contested name with two competing claimants near the hard deadline

inputs.scopeNote: Originated provisional on da-windows (#49, RATIFIED 2026-06-14): the boundary selective-reveal attack is neutralized iff the inclusivity is pinned — exactly #49 S2 (inclusive deadlines) + S3 (holdsPriority keyed to h+W), RATIFIED 2026-06-14. With the boundary pinned, the §6d (candidate) contested-priority forfeit becomes testable: a just-past-deadline claimant forfeits, and the at-boundary claimant converges on every verifier (no §3 fork). DA-window edge (h+W), distinct from the non-DA edges routed to PR-13. originated as a provisional-vector.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored from a provisional vector (decisionDeps + flipMarker shed on ratification); status locked (per-area adversarial review passed; authorityTier RATIFIED 2026-06-14 (DK ratified da-windows #49 O1 / recovery-auth #50 b1); lineage in DECISIONS #49/#50, registry Part B, decision papers, git); ratified per DK's #49 S2 boundary ruling.

expected.invariant: on a contested name a claimant whose first-servable height is past the inclusive hard deadline (h+W+1 under #49 S2) forfeits priority and is rejected; the at-boundary (h+W) claimant resolves identically on every verifier, so the boundary selective-reveal attack cannot manufacture a convergence fork (§3); identical on deterministic replay
expected.rationale: The attack — arranging first-servable exactly at the deadline to win on some implementations and lose on others — is neutralized iff boundary inclusivity is pinned, which is exactly D13/#49 S2. With #49's inclusive deadlines, the boundary verdict converges, so the contested-name priority rule (§6d, candidate) becomes testable. The verdict is derivable but depends on #49 (candidate).

sources: docs/core/B2_KERNEL_HARDENING.md D9 (lines 344-353)
docs/core/DECISIONS.md 49. da-windows S6 (lines 1069-1070) and corroboration C3 (lines 1081-1084) — RATIFIED 2026-06-14 (lines 1043-1047)
docs/spec/ONT_DATA_AVAILABILITY_AGREEMENT.md §6e S6 (lines 185-189) — 'K >= W + C ... superseded ... the weaker W <= K form ... permits include-then-retract'; §6a (lines 127-132) — stated W <= K

inputs.attackFlagText: §6a's stated constraint (W <= K) is weaker than the prototype's enforced invariant (K >= W+C): with C > 0 and W = K the challenge resolves after finalization, opening a divergence window the spec text as written permits. The hardening pass must pick the strong form explicitly.
inputs.predicateSignature: daKernelConstruct(W, C, K) -> {verdict}; daVerdict(anchor, currentHeight, W, C, K, priorKernelState) -> {verdict}

fixture.caseWeakFormTripleRejected: a parameter triple with K < W+C (e.g. K=W, C>0) -> REJECT at kernel construction (#49 S6 strong form K >= W + C is load-bearing)
fixture.caseNoIncludeThenRetract: a batch whose challenge deadline has not passed at the finalization height never finalizes -> no include-then-retract (the divergence window the weak W<=K form would permit is foreclosed)
fixture.caseEligiblePresence: the positive companion: an eligible anchor is absent from the confirmed root while tip < h+K and present once tip >= h+K
fixture.WCK: #49 provisional parameters (W=2, C=3, K=6 satisfy K >= W+C); the K=W,C>0 counterexample violates S6

inputs.scopeNote: Originated provisional on da-windows (#49, RATIFIED 2026-06-14): #49 S6 explicitly picks the strong form K >= W + C (load-bearing; the weak W<=K read alone permits include-then-retract at W=K,C>0). The verdict (a K<W+C triple is rejected at construction; eligible anchors never retract for an availability reason) is derivable from S6 but the algebra is RATIFIED 2026-06-14. originated as a provisional-vector.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored from a provisional vector (decisionDeps + flipMarker shed on ratification); status locked (per-area adversarial review passed; authorityTier RATIFIED 2026-06-14 (DK ratified da-windows #49 O1 / recovery-auth #50 b1); lineage in DECISIONS #49/#50, registry Part B, decision papers, git); ratified per DK's #49 S6 strong-form ruling.

expected.invariant: the DA kernel enforces the strong form K >= W + C (#49 S6): a (K,W,C) triple with K < W+C (e.g. K=W, C>0) is rejected at construction, and no eligible anchor retracts for an availability reason (no include-then-retract); identical on deterministic replay
expected.rationale: da-windows (#49) S6 explicitly picks the strong form K >= W + C and states it is load-bearing — the weaker W <= K read alone permits include-then-retract at W=K, C>0, which the strong form forecloses. The B2 extraction independently surfaced this (conflict C3). The verdict (a (K,W,C) triple with K < W+C is rejected at kernel construction; eligible anchors never retract for an availability reason) is derivable, but the algebra is ratified 2026-06-14.

sources: docs/core/B2_KERNEL_HARDENING.md D12 (lines 375-384), Blocked on decision: da-windows (line 379)
docs/core/DECISIONS.md 49. da-windows S5/S7 (lines 1067-1079) — parameters as inputs, two-parameterization vectors; RATIFIED 2026-06-14
docs/spec/ONT_DATA_AVAILABILITY_AGREEMENT.md §6e parameters block (lines 191-205) — provisional (6,2,3) placeholders, no constant in @ont/consensus
docs/core/SOFTWARE_INVENTORY.md STATUS parameter-table row (line 91) — 'placeholders cannot harden past candidate' [ledger discipline]

inputs.attackFlagText: Sim defaults can fossilize: vectors written against K=6/W=2/C=3 risk becoming de-facto frozen parameters — the inventory rule says placeholders cannot pass candidate.
inputs.predicateSignature: daKernelConstruct(W, C, K) -> {verdict}; daVerdict parameterized by (W,C,K) passed as inputs (no constant in @ont/consensus)

fixture.caseInvalidParamsRejected: kernel construction rejects missing or invalid parameters (non-integer, negative, or K<W+C) -> REJECT
fixture.caseTwoParameterizationTotality: the predicate is total and correct at TWO distinct (K,W,C) triples satisfying K>=W+C (e.g. (6,2,3) and (10,3,4)); a constant baked to (6,2,3) fails the second parameterization, exposing a fossilized default
fixture.params: #49 S5/S7: (K,W,C) are per-network consensus parameters passed as kernel inputs; no constant lives in @ont/consensus; provisional (6,2,3) are placeholders only

inputs.scopeNote: Originated provisional on da-windows (#49, RATIFIED 2026-06-14): #49 S5/S7 pins that (K,W,C) are per-network parameters passed to the kernel as inputs (no constant in @ont/consensus) and mandates conformance at two distinct parameterizations so a baked-in constant cannot pass. The verdict (reject invalid params; total across parameterizations; no hardcoded numbers) is derivable from S5/S7 but RATIFIED 2026-06-14. The 'placeholders cannot pass candidate' inventory rule (ratified ledger discipline) reinforces, not replaces, the #49 dependency. originated as a provisional-vector.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored from a provisional vector (decisionDeps + flipMarker shed on ratification); status locked (per-area adversarial review passed; authorityTier RATIFIED 2026-06-14 (DK ratified da-windows #49 O1 / recovery-auth #50 b1); lineage in DECISIONS #49/#50, registry Part B, decision papers, git); the two-parameterization vector's purpose changes on DK's #49 S5/S7 ruling.

expected.invariant: (K,W,C) are kernel inputs with no constant in @ont/consensus (#49 S5/S7): construction rejects missing/invalid params (non-integer, negative, K<W+C), and the predicate is total at two distinct K>=W+C parameterizations so a baked-in (6,2,3) constant fails the second; identical on deterministic replay
expected.rationale: da-windows (#49) S5/S7 pins that (K,W,C) are per-network consensus parameters passed to the kernel as inputs with no constant in @ont/consensus, and mandates conformance vectors at two distinct parameterizations so a baked-in constant cannot pass. The verdict (kernel rejects missing/invalid params; predicate is total across parameterizations satisfying K>=W+C; no hardcoded numbers) is derivable, but rests on the provisional algebra. The 'placeholders cannot pass candidate' discipline is a ratified inventory-ledger rule that reinforces, not replaces, the #49 dependency.

inputs.attackFlagText: A one-block ambiguity at the deadline is a convergence fork by construction — exactly the boundary-divergence class §3 warns about; any cross-implementation disagreement here splits roots on contested names.
inputs.predicateSignature: daVerdict(anchor, firstServableHeight, currentHeight, W[#49=2], C[#49=3], K[#49=6], priorKernelState) -> {verdict}

fixture.caseFirstServableAtHplusWplusC: a claim first-servable at exactly h+W+C -> includable -> ACCEPT (inclusive <= comparison, #49 S2)
fixture.caseBoundaryAtHplusW: first-servable at exactly h+W -> in-window -> ACCEPT (boundary vector pinning the <= comparison)
fixture.caseStrictLessThanDocumentingNegative: the documenting companion: a strict-< implementation that fails the h+W boundary vector (treats h+W as out-of-window) is non-conformant, and first-servable = h+W+C+1 -> reject
fixture.WCK: #49 provisional parameters (W=2, C=3, K=6); §6e S2 inclusive deadlines

inputs.scopeNote: Originated provisional on da-windows (#49, RATIFIED 2026-06-14): D13's verdict is candidate-stays (inclusive <= deadline comparisons), but the substantive inclusive reading is supplied by #49 S2 in §6e ('a witness at the deadline height exactly is in-window'); D13's own 'Needed spec work' still names a future §6b/§6c amendment and #49 is RATIFIED 2026-06-14. Because the <= choice rests on the provisional #49 algebra (today it exists only in sim code + the provisional §6e), the boundary verdict is provisional-vector (now ratified). DA-window edges (h+W, h+W+C), distinct from the non-DA edges routed to PR-13.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored from a provisional vector (decisionDeps + flipMarker shed on ratification); status locked (per-area adversarial review passed; authorityTier RATIFIED 2026-06-14 (DK ratified da-windows #49 O1 / recovery-auth #50 b1); lineage in DECISIONS #49/#50, registry Part B, decision papers, git); ratified per DK's #49 S2 boundary ruling.

expected.invariant: under #49 S2 inclusive comparisons a claim first-servable at exactly h+W is in-window and at exactly h+W+C is includable (both ACCEPT); h+W+C+1 is rejected; a strict-< implementation that fails the h+W boundary is non-conformant; identical on deterministic replay. DA-window edges pinned by #49 S2, not the non-DA edges routed to PR-13.
expected.rationale: D13's verdict is candidate-stays (inclusive <= deadline comparisons). The substantive inclusive reading is now provided by da-windows (#49) S2 in §6e ('a witness at the deadline height exactly is in-window'), but the rule's own 'Needed spec work' still names a future spec PR to amend §6b/§6c, and #49 is ratified 2026-06-14. Because the <= choice rests on the provisional #49 algebra (today it exists only in sim code + the provisional §6e), the boundary verdict is provisional-vector (now ratified).
